title: Visual exit left multiline parameter rows raw until cursor contact
summary: After a Visual selection ended, std and library-prefix presentation recovered but multiline function-parameter movement remained absent on formerly selected rows until j or k touched them.
details: Multiline parameter movement is rendered by aliases.lua through flip_param_line.
Visual cursor events correctly cleared aliases extmarks for selected rows.
view.lua subscribed to ModeChanged, but aliases.lua and other raw-line modules selected their own event lists and omitted ModeChanged.
Escape therefore restored the overlay module only; a later CursorMoved event repaired aliases one row at a time.
The correction makes ModeChanged a mandatory shared lifecycle event and diffs reveal sets centrally. Arrow alignment was migrated from bespoke cursor-only state to the same reveal lifecycle.
rationale: This incident is retained because it exposed an architectural ownership error rather than merely a missing case in the parameter parser.

provenance.note: Owner supplied the exact interaction and observed partial recovery behavior.
The report mapped directly to aliases missing ModeChanged while view handled it.

evidence.claim: The pre-fix regression failed identically for character, line, and block selection; it now passes all modes and the module matrix.
Mandatory ModeChanged and reveal delta logic provide the fix.

aliases: rows repair when cursor touches them
questions: Why did function arguments stay unmangled after Escape?
Why did j and k repair one stale line at a time?
Which bug motivated centralized reveal lifecycle handling?

last_verified.method: Failing regression captured before fix, then passed across all Visual modes and representative modules.
